title: ipomopsis aggregata subsp. collina
intro: Ipomopsis aggregata subsp. collina, also known as skyrocket or scarlet gilia, is a perennial plant native to the western United States, characterized by its vibrant red or purple flowers and tall, upright growth habit. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, primarily for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Ipomopsis aggregata subsp. collina include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
